title: James Comey on Trump: I refused to go away
gist: An interview with former FBI director James Comey, now prosecuted for allegedly threatening Trump's life over an Instagram post, who argues Trump is weaponising the DOJ for personal retribution against critics. Comey predicts Democrats will win a congressional majority at the midterms, that Trump's relevance will fade, and is 'deeply optimistic' about a post-Trump American recovery.

insights.claim: Comey claims Trump is openly using the Department of Justice for 'retribution missions' against critics including Letitia James, John Brennan, Adam Schiff and E Jean Carroll.
insights.speaker: Graeme Green
insights.so_what: Politicised prosecution of opponents is a rule-of-law deterioration signal with implications for US institutional-risk premia.
insights.quote: He is plain about wanting to use the power of the Department of Justice to retaliate against people who’ve been critical of him.

insights.claim: Comey is charged with threatening Trump's life — carrying a maximum 10-year sentence — over a May 2025 Instagram of seashells reading '86 47', which he says had no violent intent and which he frames as politically motivated.
insights.speaker: Graeme Green
insights.so_what: A high-profile politically-charged prosecution is a barometer of US democratic-norm erosion that markets watch for stability risk.
insights.quote: charges which carry a maximum penalty of 10 years in prison
insights.locator: stands accused of threatening the life

insights.claim: Two previous charges against Comey have already been thrown out of court, which he cites as evidence the prosecutions are politically driven rather than substantive.
insights.speaker: Graeme Green
insights.so_what: Prior dismissed charges support the politicisation narrative and suggest weak underlying cases.
insights.quote: Two previous charges have already been thrown out of court.

insights.claim: Comey expects Democrats to win a congressional majority at the midterms, after which Trump's relevance will fade and he will become a 'wounded, 80-year-old narcissist' losing the spotlight that animates him.
insights.speaker: Graeme Green
insights.so_what: A midterm Democratic majority would constrain executive action — a base case worth pricing for US policy-gridlock scenarios.
insights.quote: the president’s “relevance will be fading. He’ll be a wounded, 80-year-old narcissist
insights.locator: He expects the Democrats to form a majority

insights.claim: Comey is 'deeply optimistic' about post-Trump America, predicting a recovery and 'a U-turn that makes Hungary look like a pimple'.
insights.speaker: Graeme Green
insights.so_what: Frames the current period as a reversible illiberal episode rather than permanent regime change — a constructive long-term US thesis.
insights.quote: a U-turn that makes Hungary look like a pimple, and a recovery that’s very exciting

insights.claim: Comey does not expect Trump to run for a third term despite his threats to do so.
insights.speaker: Graeme Green
insights.so_what: A read on the constitutional-crisis tail risk of a third-term attempt — Comey discounts it.
insights.quote: Comey doesn’t expect Trump to run for a third term, as he has threatened
